_comment: M89 D1 HELD-OUT generalization corpus (finding #1, the CRITICAL anti-self-fulfilling-oracle guard). These gaps are NOT used to author/tune the classifier — they are NOVEL gaps the classifier must label correctly by FEATURE CLASS (does the gap reference a third-party/library/platform/spec proper-noun or capability, vs. a this-repo path/symbol/file), NOT by hard-coded keywords scraped from the seen 13-item corpus. A classifier that passes test/fixtures/m89-labeled-corpus.json 13/13 but mislabels ANY held-out item FAILS A1 (proves it memorized keywords instead of generalizing). None of these proper nouns (Stripe, Chrome storage-quota, CSS :has()) or repo symbols (isOrderLocked, gsd-t-verify.workflow.js, cli-preflight) appears in the seen corpus.

items.gap: What is the exact name of the Stripe webhook signature-verification header, and how is the signed payload constructed?
items.featureSignal: third-party API (Stripe) + spec/HTTP-header term — proper-noun + capability, not in this repo

items.gap: What is the per-origin storage-quota limit for the Chrome extension storage.local API?
Which browsers currently support the CSS :has() selector and from what version?
What does isOrderLocked return in this repo when the order is already locked?
items.featureSignal: this-repo local symbol (isOrderLocked) + 'this repo' anchor — grep-able

items.gap: What exit code does cli-preflight use when it hard-fails on a wrong branch?
items.featureSignal: known local symbol/file (cli-preflight) — repo's own code
